privileged_info_summary: Patient 5 from the 3D-IRCADb-01 dataset exhibits Type I (normal) hepatic vein anatomy according to the Nakamura classification. In this pattern, the Right Hepatic Vein (RHV) is the dominant right-side drainage and the Middle (MHV) and Left (LHV) hepatic veins typically form a common trunk. Anatomical studies indicate that hepatic vein ostium diameters range from 5 to 27 mm. The distance between major hepatic veins at the Inferior Vena Cava (IVC) junction typically ranges from 23 to 53 mm, and the proximity of the hepatic vein confluence to the diaphragm or IVC junction is generally between 10 and 15 mm.
